concepts.title: Halogenation of alkyne
concepts.conceptBody:
Halogenation of Alkynes

Reaction type: Electrophilic Addition 

  • Overall transformation :$\text{C}\equiv \text{C}$ to $\text{X}-\text{C}=\text{C}-\text{X}$ (and potentially to ${{\text{X}}_{2}}\text{C}-\text{C}{{\text{X}}_{2}}$ )
  • Reagent : normally the halogen (e.g. $\text{B}{{\text{r}}_{2}}$ ) in an inert solvent like methylene chloride, $\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{l}}_{2}}$.
  • Addition of ${{\text{X}}_{2}}$ is not relevant since all the new bonds are the same, C-X.
  • Reaction proceed via cyclic halonium ion.
  • Stereoselectivity : anti since the two C-X bonds form in separate steps one from ${{\text{X}}_{2}}$ the other ${{\text{X}}^{-}}$.
  • For HBr, care must be taken to avoid the formation of radicals as the alternate radical addition occurs with opposite regiochemistry.

concepts.title: Reaction of alkynes
concepts.conceptBody:
Reaction of Alkynes

1. Hydrogenation of Alkynes

Reaction Type: Electrophilic Addition 

  • Alkynes can be partially reduced to cis-alkenes with ${{\text{H}}_{2}}$ in the presence of poisoned catalysts. (eg. $\text{Pd}/\text{CaC}{{\text{O}}_{3}}/$ quinoline which is also known as Lindlar's catalyst).
  • Alkynes when reduced with lindlars catalyst gives nearly $100%$ cis isomer while Na in liquid ammonia gives nearly $100%$ trans isomer.
  • Alkynes can be reduced to alkanes with ${{\text{H}}_{2}}$ in the presence of catalyst (Pt, $\text{Pd},\text{Ni}$ etc.)
  • The new C-H $\sigma $ bonds are formed simultaneously from $\text{H}$ atoms absorbed onto the metal surface.
  • The reaction is stereospecific giving only the syn addition product.
  • The "poisoned" catalyst prevents over-reduction, which would give the alkane by reducing the alkene.

2. Addition of Boron Hydrides

Diborane, the simplest hydride of boron reacts with alkyne to form trialkenylborane. Diborane splits into two $\text{B}{{\text{H}}_{3}}$ units and the addition of $\text{B}{{\text{H}}_{3}}$ takes place following Markownikoff's rule. The addition continues as long as hydrogen is attached to boron atom.

$2\text{R}-\text{C}\equiv \text{CH}+{{\text{B}}_{2}}{{\text{H}}_{6}}\text{ }\xrightarrow{{}}2\text{R}-\text{CH}=\text{CH}-\text{B}{{\text{H}}_{2}}$

$\text{R}-\text{C}\equiv \text{CH}+\text{R}-\text{CH}=\text{CH}-\text{B}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{ }\xrightarrow{{}}{{(\text{R}-\text{CH}=\text{CH})}_{2}}\text{BH}$

$\text{R}-\text{C}\equiv \text{CH}+{{(\text{R}-\text{CH}=\text{CH})}_{2}}\text{BH }\xrightarrow{{}}{{(\text{R}-\text{CH}=\text{CH})}_{3}}~\text{B}$

Trialkenylborane on hydrolysis gives alkene.

${{(\text{R}-\text{CH}=\text{CH})}_{3}}~\text{B}\xrightarrow[hydrolysis]{C{{H}_{3}}COOH}3\text{R}-\text{CH}=\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}+\text{B}{{(\text{OH})}_{3}}$

Internal alkynes give rise to alkenes where geometrical isomerism is possible. Hydroboration followed by hydrolysis of alkynes gives cis alkene as the major product.

Oxidation of trialkenylborane with alkaline ${{\text{H}}_{2}}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$ results in the formation of carbonyl compounds. Terminal alkynes give rise to aldehydes whereas internal alkynes give rise to ketones.

3. Dimerisation

Acetylene dimerises when treated with a mixture of $\text{C}{{\text{u}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{l}}_{2}}$ and $\text{N}{{\text{H}}_{4}}\text{Cl}$ to give vinyl acetylene.

$2\text{CH}\equiv \text{CH}\xrightarrow{C{{u}_{2}}c{{l}_{2}}+N{{H}_{4}}Cl}\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}=\text{CH}-\text{C}\equiv \text{CH}$

The dimer undergoes addition reactions just like any other unsaturated hydrocarbon. The addition reaction preferably takes place at the triple bond and not at the double bond inspite of the fact that alkynes are less reactive than alkenes towards electrophilic addition reactions. For example, addition of $\text{HCl}$ to vinyl acetylene gives chloroprene.

4. Oxidation

Alkynes are oxidised by hot alkaline $\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$, which causes cleavage of $-\text{C}\equiv \text{C}-$ resulting in the formation of salts of carboxylic acids. The salts on acidification are converted into acids. Internal alkynes give mixture of carboxylic acids while terminal alkynes give a carboxylic acid and the terminal C-atom is oxidised to $\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}$ and ${{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$.

$\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}-\text{C}\equiv \text{CH}\xrightarrow[\left( ii \right){{H}^{+}}]{\left( i \right)K{{M}_{n}}{{O}_{4}}/O{{H}^{-}}/\Delta }\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\text{COOH}+\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}+{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}$

$\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}-\text{C}=\text{C}-\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\xrightarrow[(ii){{H}^{+}}]{\left( i \right)K{{M}_{n}}{{O}_{4}}/OH/\Delta }\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\text{COOH}+\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{3}}\text{C}{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{COOH}$

5. Polymerisation Reactions

(i) Hydrochloric acid adds to acetylene in the presence of$\text{H}{{\text{g}}^{2+}}$ion as catalyst to form vinyl chloride. Polymerisation of vinyl chloride results in the formation of polyvinyl chloride (PVC).

(ii) Addition of$\text{HCN}$ to ethyne is catalysed by $\text{C}{{\text{u}}_{2}}\text{C}{{\text{l}}_{2}}$ in $\text{HCl}$. The product obtained is acrylonitrile, which on polymerisation gives polyacrylonitrile (PAN).

Acrylonitrile is also used in the manufacture of a synthetic rubber called BuNa-N (a copolymer of butadiene and acrylonitrile) and a thermoplastic called ABS (a terpolymer of acrylonitrile, butadiene and styrene).

(iii) Acetic acid adds to ethyne in the presence of $\text{H}{{\text{g}}^{2+}}$ ion to give vinyl acetate, which is used as monomer in the preparation of polyvinyl acetate (PVA).

6. Reaction with hot iron tube

  • Acetylene when passed over red hot iron tube forms benzene.

This reaction involves a cyclic transition state in which$8{{\text{e}}^{-}}$participate. Such compounds have aromatic character as aromatic compounds are relatively more stable. So these transition states are more stable.

7. Addition of Hypohalous acid

Alkynes react with hypohalous acid in the molar ratio of 1: 2 to give dihalo Ketones.

Acetylene forms dihaloaldehyde

examples.title: Which of the following used for the conversion of 2-hexyne into trans-2-hexane

The number of $\pi$-bonds in the product formed by passing acetylene through dilute sulphuric acid containing mercuric sulphate is
The bond length between the hybridised carbon atom and other carbon atom is minimum in
Number of acidic hydrogen atoms in butyne-1 are

Which of the following reactions will yield 2, 2-dibromopropane
Which will undergo reaction with ammoniacal $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$
